title: Markets plunge wiping tens of billions off super balances | The Business | ABC NEWS
description: The war in the Middle East is escalating, with more attacks on critical energy infrastructure driving oil and gas prices higher.  The shock and awe has been a reality check for financial markets.  Tens of billions of dollars was wiped off the S&P/ASX 200 on Thursday.  The Australian share market has now lost roughly 7 per cent of its value ($250 billion) since the war began.  Anyone with superannuation is exposed to movements in financial markets.  Super Ratings director, Kirby Rappell, says now is when people start to worry whether they should be move their money to cash, saying "I think that we always find when we see this sort of discussion around a lot of market volatility, people being tempted to switch into cash. I think you can call your super fund and have a chat and sort of understand or get some insights from them or even some advice. Typically, moving to cash is easy. Figuring out when to move back is hard." Director of private credit at Aura Group, Brett Craig, says "be careful not to sell out at the bottom and crystallize your loss. hold your nerve, markets will, as we've seen in the past, revert to the mean." The process of moving your super savings away from shares and more towards cash can take a little as couple of days but experts warn people should seek their own personal financial advice before making major investment decisions.  #ABCBusiness  Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: ASX falls sharply as oil price surges to four-year high | Finance Report | ABC NEWS
description: The Australian share market has wiped out all its gains since November 2025 as hopes for a quick resolution to the Middle East war fade.  Meanwhile, the price of Brent crude oil has surged above $US112 per barrel, its highest in four years, after a major escalation in Israel-Iran hostilities.  US Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell says he'll remain on the Fed's rate-setting board as a governor, even after his term ends in May. The decision is likely to impede Donald Trump's campaign for lower interest rates, as finance presenter David Chau explains. title: BOM declares cyclone watch for NT, Tropical Cyclone Narelle set to hit on Saturday | ABC NEWS
description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle is expected to cross into the NT from late Saturday, with Groote Eylandt, Numbulwar, Borroloola, Ngukurr, Gapuwiyak and Bulman now issued a cyclone watch.  Hundreds of people from the remote community of Numbulwar are in the process of being evacuated to Darwin.  Authorities are urging residents of NT communities in the cyclone's path to start preparing now. Courtney Barrett Peters reports for ABC News Northern Territory. title: Shashi Tharoor says India "won't burn bridges" with Iran over Middle East War | The World | ABC News
description: India's foreign policy doctrine of friend to all, enemy to none - is facing a severe test with the war in the Middle East. Prime Minister Narendra Modi has publicly hailed India's friendship with the leaders of Israel, the US and Iran, but had little to say about the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.   So where does India really stand on this war?    Indian opposition MP and former senior United Nations official, Dr Shashi Tharoor spoke to The World's Girish Sawlani about India's delicate diplomatic handling of the conflict.
